source.description: Six-panel contact sheet generated for a pencil-and-colored-pencil rural mailbox sequence, then cropped into lesson assets.
source.notes: Created as the June 2, 2026 honest backdated Sketcha.day archive lesson.

process_strategy: The frames use one mailbox pose and progressively add the rounded box, post, raised flag, door seam, mail slot, grass, and final restrained shading.

final_elements.name: rounded mailbox body
final_elements.description: A classic curved-top mailbox box facing slightly to the side.

final_elements.name: front door curve
final_elements.description: A curved front door line that shows the mailbox opening.

final_elements.name: support post
final_elements.description: A simple vertical wooden post holding the mailbox above the ground.

final_elements.name: raised side flag
final_elements.description: A small upright mail flag attached to the side of the box.

final_elements.name: door handle
final_elements.description: A tiny handle mark on the front door of the mailbox.

final_elements.name: mail slot seam
final_elements.description: A subtle seam and side line that clarify the mailbox form.

final_elements.name: tufts of grass
final_elements.description: Small grass shapes around the base of the post.

final_elements.name: soft blue and red shading
final_elements.description: Light blue-gray shading on the mailbox and muted red on the raised flag.

frames.visible_job: Adds the rounded mailbox body as a light construction shape with the curve kept simple.
Adds the front door curve and the support post underneath the existing mailbox body.
Places the side flag on the already drawn mailbox and refines the body edges.
Adds the door handle and mail slot seam while clarifying the mailbox contours.
Adds tufts of grass plus light blue-gray and red shading on already placed forms.

final_step.visible_job: Only darkens keeper lines, clarifies the flag and hardware, and deepens the existing shading and grass.

rejection_checks: No mailbox body, post, flag, handle, grass, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same mailbox angle, scale, and placement as the step frames.
